title: TrialSchema Export
description: A portable, agent-friendly representation of clinical-trial eligibility designed for automated patient matching. TrialSchema makes explicit what to check (criteria concepts and assertions), where to look (document types, default visit order, and criterion-level routing), how to evaluate (constraints, thresholds, polarity, rank, and fallbacks), and which normalized care path a trial belongs to (care_path_ids). Domain-neutral (oncology, cardiology, metabolic, rare disease, ...) and standards-aligned: a deliberate subset of HL7 FHIR R5 ResearchStudy with eligibility-focused additions, FHIR-shaped Codings, UCUM units, and an explicit user-defined document routing profile. Free-text fallbacks remain valid everywhere so end users never have to hand-curate ontology codes.

format_version.description: Semantic version of the format. Minor versions are additive.

routing_profile.description: The export-level answer to where a matching agent may look: available patient-document types, default scan set, and default visit order.

care_path_catalog.description: Optional normalized care paths active when this export was generated. Trials reference these via care_path_ids as a first-pass matching filter before criterion evaluation. Persisted so English display names, assignments, and original source-value aliases survive a re-import.

Extensions.description: Free-form namespaced extension bag. Implementers SHOULD prefix keys with their own reverse-DNS namespace, e.g. "org.example.foo".

RoutingProfile.description: The document universe that answers where to look. It defines available patient-document types, the default maximum scan set for most criteria, and the default visit order used when a criterion has no more specific routing.

document_type_system.description: Canonical URI for the document-type id namespace.

default_scan_set.description: Default maximum document set to scan for most criteria. Agents should inspect these document types first, ordered by default_visit_order, unless criterion.routing gives a more specific primary/fallback route.

default_visit_order.description: General order in which a matching agent should visit patient documents. Criterion-level routing may narrow the set, but this order remains the tie-breaker.

RoutingDocumentType.description: A patient-document type available to the extractor and downstream matching agent. Labels and hints explain what content is usually found there; order lives in routing_profile.default_visit_order, while criterion-level routing states where each rule should look.

hint.description: Agent-facing guidance describing typical content and when to scan this document type.

Coding.description: FHIR-shaped reference to a concept in a terminology. Every field is optional; `text` alone is a valid free-text fallback so end users are never required to fill in ontology codes.

system.description: Canonical URI of the terminology, e.g. http://loinc.org, http://snomed.info/sct, http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm, http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10, http://www.genenames.org

display.description: Human-readable label as it appears in the terminology.

text.description: User-facing free-text label. Always allowed; required when neither system+code nor display are set.

Quantity.description: FHIR-shaped numeric quantity. UCUM is recommended for `system`+`code`; `unit` is the human-readable label.

code.description: UCUM (or other system) code, e.g. "a" for years, "mg/dL", "L/min".

id.description: Stable primary key. Typically an NCT id; any unique string is allowed.

enabled.description: Local TrialSchema matching switch. If false, agents should skip this trial regardless of dates, care path, or criteria. This is not a recruitment status.

conditions.description: Trial-level scope: ClinicalTrials.gov-style conditions, diagnoses, disorders, or study focus. This is not by itself an eligibility rule; explicit patient requirements still belong in criteria.

interventions.description: Trial-level scope: investigational or protocol interventions such as drugs, devices, procedures, radiation, diagnostics, or behavioral interventions. This is not by itself an eligibility rule; prior/current therapy requirements still belong in criteria.

lifecycle.$comment: FHIR R5: ResearchStudy.period. TrialSchema v1 keeps only matcher-relevant open/close dates.

start_date.description: Optional matching-window open date. If present and in the future, agents should not match patients before this date.

completion_date.description: Optional matching-window close date. If present and in the past, agents should skip this trial.

care_path_ids.description: Optional but first-class trial-level matching filter. Ids of the normalized care paths (from care_path_catalog) this trial belongs to, for example breast_cancer or heart_failure. Matching agents should evaluate criteria only when the patient care path matches at least one of these ids; if omitted, care-path filtering cannot be applied. Persisted so manual assignments survive a re-import.

criteria.description: Ordered, agent-friendly eligibility rules. Each item should represent one patient-level inclusion or exclusion requirement and make explicit what to check, how to evaluate it, and where to look. Do not use criteria for mere trial scope such as a listed condition, intervention, or care-path assignment. Array index === run order; the first criterion runs first.
criteria.$comment: FHIR R5: ResearchStudy.recruitment.eligibility (resolved). v1 represents each criterion inline rather than via a Group reference.

PartialDate.description: ISO-8601 date with optional precision: YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD. Empty string is allowed for editable drafts.

CarePathCatalogEntry.description: A normalized care path in the export-level enum, used as a first-pass matching filter before evaluating criteria. TrialSchema v1 stores stable care-path references such as breast_cancer, heart_failure, or type_2_diabetes; detailed pathway phases or actions can be modeled in extensions or future versions when needed. Trials reference the path by id via care_path_ids. The id and label are English; aliases capture original source values and cross-language spellings used for auto-assignment.

aliases.description: Original source care-path values plus synonyms/spellings used for alias-based auto-assignment.

concept.description: Optional condition-domain anchor (typically a SNOMED CT code).

id.description: Stable identifier within the trial. Default convention: "INC-01", "EXC-01", …

enabled.description: User toggle. Disabled criteria MUST be skipped by the matcher.

rank.description: 1..N position in the run order. Lower runs first, allowing a matching agent to check cheap or decisive criteria earlier.

source_text.description: Verbatim original criterion text in the source language. Immutable after extraction; do not overwrite it with a clarified, translated, or manually rewritten version.

clarified_text.description: Optional agent-facing rewrite of the same criterion, typically in English, made explicit and unambiguous for patient matching. It MUST preserve the exact clinical meaning, thresholds, negation, and logic of source_text. Consumers SHOULD prefer `clarified_text ?? source_text` for matching while retaining source_text for audit, debugging, and reprocessing.

assertion.description: Whether the criterion asserts the concept's presence (`present`), its absence (`absent`, e.g. "no history of …"), or makes no assertion (`unknown`).

guidance.description: Free-text guidance for the matching agent when source_text, clarified_text, concept, assertion, constraint, and routing are not enough. Strongly recommended when the routing includes "other".

Concept.description: What the criterion is about. This answers what to check and is decoupled from where to look (which lives in routing) and how to evaluate (which lives in constraint/assertion).

domain.description: High-level clinical category. Influences how the matcher fetches data.

code.description: Best-effort code in a recognised terminology (LOINC for observations, SNOMED CT for conditions/procedures, RxNorm/ATC for medications, HGNC for genes, ICD-10/-11 for diagnoses). Optional.

text.description: Human-readable concept name (e.g. "Creatinine clearance", "ECOG performance status"). Always allowed.

Constraint.description: Tagged union describing how the criterion evaluates against patient data: comparison, range, code-set, boolean, existence, or text fallback. Use `kind: "text"` as a graceful fallback when no machine-evaluable form is available.

oneOf.description: Free-text fallback. Always valid; the matcher will treat the criterion as LLM-judgement-required.

Routing.description: Criterion-specific answer to where to look. It references the export's top-level routing_profile and tells the matcher which patient-record document types to search first (`primary`) before optional secondary routes (`fallback`). Empty arrays mean no criterion-specific preference; use routing_profile.default_scan_set first, ordered by routing_profile.default_visit_order.

DocumentType.description: Document type id declared in routing_profile.document_types[].id. Default ids use system https://trialschema.org/v1/document-types, but profiles may define additional ids.

extracted_by.description: Identifier of the model / tool that produced this criterion, e.g. "openai/gpt-4o-mini" or "manual".

TrialProvenance.description: Trial-level edit provenance. Records whether the trial was structured by AI and/or hand-edited by a reviewer, so a resume/merge of a newer raw upload can preserve reviewed manual work instead of redoing it.

ai_processed.description: True if an AI model produced or updated this trial.

manually_edited.description: True if a human reviewer edited this trial in the workspace.

ai_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent AI update.

ai_by.description: Identifier of the AI model / tool, e.g. "openai/gpt-4o-mini" or "manual-paste".

edited_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent manual edit.
